title: Wicket Wystri Warrick
content:
{{Top}} {{Update|[[The Kroolok]], [[The Woklings' Tale]], [[Galactic Tales: Lost in the Woods]], [[The Princess and the Scoundrel]], [[Traps and Tribulations]], [[R2-D2 is LOST!]], [[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition]], [[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: A Junior Novel]], [[Star Wars Helmet Collection 70]], [[Star Wars Bestiary, Vol. 1: Creatures of the Galaxy]]}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Wicket-2024Base.png]] |option1=35 ABY |image2=[[File:Wicket_RotJ.png]] |option2=4 ABY |name=Wicket Wystri Warrick |homeworld=[[Endor]]{{Film|VI}} |birth=[[7 BBY]], Endor{{Instagram|username=cosbaby|url=p/DIOFf_Hv-mY|name=[[Hot Toys|Cosbaby By Hot Toys]]|date=2025-04-11|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/Le3Tz}} |death= |species=[[Ewok]]{{Databank|wicket-w-warrick|Wicket W. Warrick}} |gender=[[Gender|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=0.85 [[meter]]s (2 [[Foot|ft]], 8 in) |mass= |hair=[[Color|Blondish-brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Brownish brown |skin= |cyber= |families=[[Warrick family]] |parents=*[[Deej]]{{StoryCite|story=The Kroolok|book=Life Day Treasury}} *[[Wicket Wystri Warrick's mother]] |partners=[[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] {{C|[[Marriage|wife]]}} |siblings=*[[Weechee (Ewok)|Weechee]] *[[Widdle Warrick]] *[[Winda]] |children=[[Pommet Warrick]] |affiliation=*[[Bright Tree tribe]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Yub nub!|Wicket Wystri Warrick|}} '''Wicket Wystri Warrick''' (sometimes shortened to '''Wicket W. Warrick''') was a [[Gender|male]] [[Ewok]] who served as a [[scout]] and [[Soldier|warrior]] on the "forest moon" of [[Endor]], a wild, temperate moon which orbited the massive [[Endor (planet)|gas giant of the same name]]. Wicket maintained a close and vital connection with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]], and was instrumental in their victory against the [[Galactic Empire]], who had laid siege to his homeworld during the [[Battle of Endor]]. By [[35 ABY]], Wicket had fathered a [[Reproduction|son]], [[Pommet Warrick|Pommet]], with the young family witnessing the [[uprising on Endor]]. ==Biography== ===Young warrior=== Wicket Wystri Warrick{{HelmetCollectionCite|70|Databank A-Z}} was an [[Ewok]] of [[Bright Tree Village]] on the forested [[moon]] [[Endor]]. As a [[wokling]], Wicket learned survival skills, and even tracked a [[troll]] through a blizzard.{{StoryCite|story=One Normal Day|book=From a Certain Point of View: Return of the Jedi}} While he was still a young Ewok, the [[Galactic Empire]], ruled by [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]], landed on Wicket’s homeworld to construct a [[Death Star's shield generator|shield generator]] in order to defend the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station|second Death Star]]. Despite any differences between Ewok and [[Human]] battle strategies, language, technology or available [[fire]]power (as well as physical differences), the [[Imperial Military|Imperial forces]] on Endor were defeated, largely due to Wicket’s knowledge of the terrain and local forests aiding in strategizing a successful attack. ===Rebel encounters=== {{Quote|You're a jittery little thing, aren't you?|Leia Organa|}} [[File:WicketMeetLeia-ROTJ.png|thumb|right|250px|Wicket meets Princess Leia Organa.]] Large-scale Imperial activity continued on Endor as construction on the new [[Death]] [[Star]] progressed. Meanwhile, the Rebel Alliance had sent a [[Endor strike team|strike team]] to the forest moon to deactivate the shield protecting the giant weapon. One day, Wicket came across the crash site of a pair of Imperial [[74-Z speeder bike|speeder bikes]]. [[Gender|He]] cautiously approached the only survivor, a [[human]] woman, prodding her with his [[spear]] until she regained consciousness. This woman was [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]], [[Princess]] of [[Alderaan]] and a leader of the Rebel Alliance. Wicket remained leery until the woman offered him some [[Foodstuff|food]] to [[Diet|eat]]. He found the food to be very good, but he again became fearful of Organa, though she was merely removing her helmet. He began to inspect the headgear but stopped and grabbed his spear when he heard more Imperials. When the Imperial [[scout trooper]]s fired at them, Wicket and Organa hid behind a log until another blast hit. At that point Wicket rolled out of sight. Then, as a [[scout]] [[trooper]] approached Organa at gunpoint, Wicket realized she was no friend of the Imperials, so he attacked the trooper's [[Limb|legs]], giving Organa a chance to swing at the trooper, grab her [[blaster]], and shoot the other Imperial before he could get away. As they headed off, Wicket urged Organa to follow him to his home [[Bright Tree Village|village]]. [[File:ABeautifulFriendship-SWL110.png|thumb|250px|left|Wicket celebrates defeating the scout troopers.]] On the way home, Wicket and Leia came upon two [[stormtrooper]]s who had stopped two other Ewoks and were attempting to arrest them. Wicket climbed a nearby [[tree]] and created a trap by tying a lasso from a vine. He then lowered the lasso over the stormtroopers, snaring them. Wicket jumped from the tree, holding the other end of the vine in order to hang the stormtroopers from the tree; however, Wicket was not heavy enough to pull the stormtroopers. Organa assisted, grabbing hold of Wicket, causing the stormtroopers to rise and be knocked unconscious by a tree branch. Wicket, Organa, and the two other Ewoks continued to Bright Tree Village. Upon arriving at the village, Wicket presented Organa with a [[Clothing|dress]] as a gift for helping him save his fellow villagers.{{FOD|Ewok Escape}} Meanwhile, other Ewoks had captured and brought in the rest of the Rebels: [[Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca]], and the [[droid]]s [[R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO]]. The Ewoks treated C-3PO as a [[Deity|god]], and the Ewok [[shaman]] [[Logray]] insisted that the other Rebels be sacrificed in the droid's honor. The proceedings were halted when Skywalker used [[the Force]] to [[Telekinesis|lift]] C-3PO into the air and convince the Ewoks that it was a show of the droid's "magic." This frightened the Ewoks, including Wicket, and caused them to run away, but they soon returned and untied the Rebels under Logray's orders. Once freed, R2-D2 used one of his [[Arc welder|built-in devices]] to deliver a mild electrical shock to [[Teebo]] in retaliation. ===The Battle of Endor=== That night, the Rebels had an audience with the Ewok leaders in hopes of encouraging them to join their cause. C-3PO animatedly told the Rebels' tale. At the end of the meeting, [[Chief]] [[Chirpa]] declared that the Rebels would become part of their [[Bright Tree tribe|tribe]]. Wicket embraced [[Captain]] Solo as the Ewoks welcomed their new comrades. [[File:Wicket_and_Rebels.png|thumb|right|250px|Wicket consults with Paploo about how to help Organa, Solo, and Chewbacca.]] Wicket and another Ewok, [[Paploo]], then joined with Solo, Organa, Chewbacca, and the droids to meet up with the Rebel [[commando]]s. They arrived a ridge overlooking the Imperial landing platform, and from there Wicket and Paploo led them to a "secret" entrance on the other side of the ridge. There, as Paploo distracted the scout [[troop]]ers to allow the Rebels to sneak inside the [[SLD-26 planetary shield generator|shield generator]] bunker, Wicket stayed behind with the droids. He remained there until they spotted more Imperials arriving; realizing the Rebels were in trouble, Wicket ran to get help. After the Rebels were led back out of the bunker at gunpoint, Wicket returned—and he brought with him an entire army of Ewoks. The [[Battle of Endor]] had begun. The Ewoks attacked with a variety of weapons and traps. Wicket joined a group hunting with bolas, and while his own attack backfired and knocked Wicket to the ground, the only injury was to his ego. The battle raged on, and the Rebels tried to make their way back into the bunker. The Rebels eventually destroyed the shield generator, allowing the [[Rebel Alliance Navy|fleet]] above the [[planet]] to destroy the Death Star, and the body of Emperor Palpatine along with it. As the victors celebrated on Endor, Solo and Organa shared an intimate moment—one which Wicket did not hesitate interrupting to share in the jubilation. ===After the battle=== {{Quote|See that? Our friends did that.''"
"''Princess Leia? See-Threepio?|Wicket Warrick and Pommet Warrick, upon seeing the ''Resurgent''-class Star Destroyer destroyed|''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition]]''}} [[File:Wicket-trooperhelmet.png|thumb|left|250px|Wicket with the helmet of a fallen stormtrooper]] At a village-wide party where the Ewoks and Rebels celebrated their victory in the battle, Wicket danced and fraternized with R2-D2. When the Alliance detected [[Imperial outpost (Endor)|Imperial holdouts]] on the other side of the moon, Wicket and at least five other Ewoks accompanied the [[Special Forces Trackers Unit|strike team]] out to the site. The Ewoks' curiosity about all the technology and buttons within the [[shuttle]] got on the nerves of [[pilot]] [[Shara Bey]] as she was flying the craft. The strike team quickly [[Assault on an Imperial outpost|took]] the base and discovered numerous Imperial comm records and plans in the wake of the Emperor's death.[[Shattered Empire 1|''Shattered Empire'' 1]] Wicket gave Organa an acorn for a [[Sanctuary tree|serpent's puzzle]] [[plant]] as a gift before she left Endor. At Skywalker's suggestion, the sapling became a part of her [[meditation]] exercises.''[[Aftermath: Life Debt]]'' Eventually, Wicket [[Reproduction|sired]] a son, named [[Pommet Warrick]]. In [[35 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']] to one [[Standard year|year]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars'': Episode VII ''The Force Awakens'']]. As ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''The Force Awakens'' to [[34 ABY]], the events of ''The Rise of Skywalker'' must occur in [[35 ABY]]. Wicket and Pommet watched as one of the [[First Order]] [[Resurgent-class Star Destroyer|Star Destroyers]] was [[Uprising on Endor|destroyed]] by a [[Unidentified heavy freighter|heavy freighter]] via the "[[Holdo maneuver]],"''[[Star Wars: Battles that Changed the Galaxy]]'' as the inhabitants of [[the galaxy]] had been inspired by the [[Resistance]]'s [[Battle of Exegol|victory]] against the resurrected Darth Sidious' [[Sith Eternal military|forces]] to [[Uprising against the First Order|rise up]].{{Film|IX}} ==Behind the scenes== ===''Return of the Jedi''=== Wicket was portrayed by [[Warwick Davis]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] ([[1983]]). The then 11-year-old actor came to be involved in the film after his grandmother heard a radio ad calling for short actors.{{InsiderCite|31|Wicket Unleashed}} When Davis' mother called to inquire on his behalf, she was told that [[Lucasfilm]] already had enough actors; however, when she mentioned that Warwick was a mere 2 feet, 11 inches tall, they reconsidered him as a possibility for a younger Ewok. Davis then met with production assistant Pat Carr at [[Elstree Studios]], where he was measured for a costume and did not even have to audition.{{InsiderCite|67|Beyond the Valley of the Ewoks}} Davis began work on the film in [[January]] [[1982]]. Originally just another Ewok, Davis caught the eye of [[George Lucas]] with aspects of his performance, such as his interaction with R2-D2 and the ability to stick his tongue through his Ewok mask. Another part of his performance, inquisitively tilting his head, was inspired by Davis' dog. Davis was later taken to America for location shooting. The day [[Kenny Baker]] was supposed to shoot the scene in which his character (originally called "Wickett") meets Princess Leia for the first time, Baker fell ill with food poisoning, and so Davis was called in as a replacement. Davis' Ewok eventually became "Wicket," while Baker's became "Paploo." Evidence of the switch remains in the [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (1983)|comic book adaptation]] of ''Return of the Jedi'', where the Ewok stealing the speeder bike is still called Wicket. As for Wicket's voice in the final film, according to Davis, it was performed by a [[Wikipedia:Native Americans in the United States|Native American]] woman.{{InsiderCite|39|When Artoo Met Wicket}} According to [[Ben Burtt]], the actress's name was [[Adeal Crooms]], and though she was not a professional, she was a talented performer.[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']] Since George Lucas' daughter [[Amanda Lucas|Amanda]] was a big fan of Wicket, Davis performed the character at a birthday party for her, delivering presents and even letting the kids force-feed him cake.{{InsiderCite|62|Return to Endor: The Stars of the Ewok Films Remember Their Adventure}} Concurrent to the shooting of ''Return of the Jedi'', first assistant director [[David Tomblin]] shot a short film on [[Wikipedia:16 mm film|16 mm film]] starring Davis called ''[[Return of the Ewok]]''. It tells an alternate tale of Warwick Davis landing the role of an Ewok and the trying to find his way to the "green moon of Endor," stopping by [[Boba Fett's Palace|Jabba's Palace]] and [[Yoda]]'s hut along the way. Davis is in his Wicket costume for much of the film. A short clip, where [[Boba Fett]] is pursuing Davis/Wicket on the second Death Star, can be seen in the blooper reel Easter egg on the [[2004]] bonus [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] for the [[original trilogy]].{{InsiderCite|46|Return of the Ewok (article)}} ===Other appearances=== Davis reprised his role for the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] TV movies ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'' ([[1984]]) and ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'' ([[1985]]), and later for the [[canon]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']] ([[2019]]). For the latter, a new Wicket costume had to be built, since the original costume and design had been lost. Costume designers used Davis' personal photos for reference in recreating the costume.{{WebCite|url=https://www.syfy.com/syfywire/warwick-davis-brought-his-son-to-play-the-other-ewok-in-star-wars-the-rise-of-skywalker|text=Warwick Davis brought his son to play the other Ewok in Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker|author=Zakarin, Jordan|date=2020-01-09|archivedate=20200622231818}} Wicket was also a main character in the Legends animated TV series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] from 1985–[[1986]], a related [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|14-issue comic book series]] from [[Marvel Comics|Marvel's]] [[Star Comics]] imprint, and a number of [[:Category:Ewok Adventure books|children's books]]. The animated series and comics introduced Wicket's family and friends, including his love interest [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka|Princess Kneesaa]], although they would not enter canon until much later. ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' established that Wicket's middle name is Wystri. On the April 27, 2008, edition of Comedy Central's ''Colbert Report'', Stephen Colbert (a self-described geek) "ate" Wicket (a large teddy bear with an apple stuffed in its mouth), reneging on a promise not to eat Ewoks. On June 7, 2013, it was announced that, in honor of the 30th anniversary of ''Star Wars'' Episode VI: ''Return of the Jedi''{{'s}} release, Bean Bunny was selected to represent Wicket Warrick for the ''Star Wars''/''The Muppets'' crossover line.{{EndorExpress|url=2013/06/star-wars-muppets-bean-bunny|text=Star Wars Muppets: Bean Bunny}} In the animated microseries ''[[Star Wars Forces of Destiny]]'', the voice of Wicket is provided by [[Dee Bradley Baker]].''[[Star Wars Forces of Destiny: Volume 1]]'' Credits'' ===Contradictions=== When Wicket and several other Ewoks were given Cosbaby merchandize by [[Hot Toys]], Logray, someone older than Wicket by several decades, was mistakenly identified as Wicket's lover on Instagram.{{Instagram|username=cosbaby|url=p/DITPAiAv_e1|name=[[Hot Toys|Cosbaby By Hot Toys]]|date=2025-04-11|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/2uVIM}} ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox| *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Defense]]'' *''[[Forces of Destiny: The Leia Chronicles]]'' *''[[Forces of Destiny: The Leia Chronicles & The Rey Chronicles]]'' *''[[The Adventures of Luke Skywalker, Jedi Knight]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Original Trilogy – A Graphic Novel]]'' *[[Bounty Hunters 34|''Bounty Hunters'' 34]] {{C|Variant cover only}} *[[Star Wars (2020) 40|''Star Wars'' (2020) 40]] {{C|Variant cover only}} *{{StoryCite|story=The Kroolok|book=Life Day Treasury}} *{{StoryCite|story=The Woklings' Tale|book=Return of the Jedi – Ewoks 1}} *{{Film|VI}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' junior novelization]] *''[[Return of the Jedi: Beware the Power of the Dark Side!]]'' {{Ab|Return of the Jedi: Beware the Power of the Dark Side!
